item.title: Ideas for GnoponEmacs
item.description:
Ideas for GnoponEmacs

  • Handle [Anchor Text|id](Anchor Text|id) links, where ID is one of the IDs in the library
    • This probably will require a local sqlite database for querying those.
    • Which means new skills - and I guess elisp that lets us use our skills?
    • Okay, so, side-idea: M-x gpe/skills/* that lets us run the skills in /.codex/skills/.
    • Which lines up with “GnoponEmacs is how human agents use an Agential Semioverse”
  • Add markdown-vault navigation that can list files by tag, dependency, or link.
  • Surface interactive-semioverse mappings for markdown files (things, interaction terms, footprints).
  • Add a vault index viewer that opens the generated index files for quick navigation.

item.title: GnoponEmacs Interface
item.description:
GnoponEmacs Interface

GnoponEmacs is the Emsemioverse’s baseline Emacs interface for human and agent work.

Scope

This interface defines the defaults for writing, navigation, and light automation in the Emsemioverse.

Notes

GnoponEmacs is not a specification repository; it is a working interface and may evolve alongside the rest of the semioverse.

item.title: GnoponEmacs
item.description:
GnoponEmacs

This configuration is named GnoponEmacs.

GnoponEmacs is a human-agent interface for working inside the Emsemioverse.

Goals

  • Prefer simple, composable packages over monoliths.
  • Keep the configuration usable in terminal-first workflows.
  • Support text production as a first-class activity.
  • Provide a clean path for future spec-aware validation and helpers.

Text Production Baseline

The baseline includes Markdown and writing-focused tooling, plus common data formats used in specifications.

  • Markdown: markdown-mode
  • Focused writing: olivetti, visual-fill-column
  • Data formats: yaml-mode, toml-mode, json-mode
  • Editing helpers: edit-indirect for fenced block editing

Future Extensions

Spec-aware behavior (linting, front-matter templates, validation) should be added as separate, opt-in layers that interpret buffer context and applicable specifications.
